runs.label: Atelier Fuego
runs.nextAction: Run observer after hero rebuild and compare against Design DNA thresholds

documents.designMarkdown:
# DESIGN.md — Atelier Fuego

## Project Framing

- Project: Atelier Fuego
- Type: premium creative studio
- Mode: autonomous
- Goal: launch a homepage that feels editorial, warm, cinematic, and clearly premium without drifting into generic agency aesthetics

## Brand Intent

- Present the studio as selective, sharp, and tactile
- Signal modernity through motion and systems, not through generic SaaS UI tropes
- Keep warmth and luxury without collapsing into sterile fashion minimalism

## Design Principles

1. Every section needs a visible compositional decision, not just balanced content blocks
2. Typography carries identity before decoration does
3. Depth must be perceptible in screenshots, not only in CSS
4. Motion should reveal hierarchy and rhythm, not act as ambient filler

## Tone And Atmosphere

- Warm-black canvas
- Editorial pacing
- Dense but breathable hero
- Grain and amber highlights
- Premium, not playful

## Composition Constraints

- Ban symmetric `1fr 1fr` hero layouts
- Require one visible overlap or container break per section
- Avoid repeating the same layout family in adjacent sections
- Hero must include a structural visual that reads immediately at desktop and mobile

## Typography Rules

- Display: expressive serif or premium grotesk
- Body: neutral but refined sans
- Scale contrast: at least 4x from body to dominant headline
- Tracking must be intentional on labels, nav, and metadata

## Palette And Token Intent

- Near-black base with warm white text
- Accent family should stay inside amber / copper / burnished gold territory
- Use soft opacity layers instead of flat gray panels

## Motion Rules

- Entrance choreography must use at least two easing personalities
- One scroll-linked motion system per hero
- Magnetic behavior reserved for primary intent actions
- No generic fade-up-only sections

## Responsive Rules

- Mobile layouts must preserve the same core tension, not merely stack content
- Tablet must be explicitly composed, not treated as a stretched mobile view
- Hero visual remains readable even when secondary detail zones collapse

## Accessibility Rules

- Contrast failures are never auto-approved
- Focus-visible on every interactive
- Heading hierarchy must remain coherent after section assembly

## Anti-Patterns

- Purple gradients on white
- Inter/Roboto/system UI defaults
- Flat single-color backgrounds with centered copy
- Thin accent-line heroes with no structural visual
- Decorative motion that does not change reading order or depth

## Seed Policy

- Allowed hero families: editorial-grid, ambient-canvas, split-stage, cinematic-overlay
- Allowed nav families: adaptive-contextual, floating-glass, fullscreen-editorial
- Banned hero patterns: centered generic SaaS hero, low-opacity dark photo on dark canvas, empty luxury minimalism
- Mutation budget: high on typography, copy layout, and detail systems; medium on core composition; low on signature-defining motion gestures
- Reference policy: borrow structure and rhythm, never clone surface styling
documents.decisionsMarkdown:
# Decisions — Atelier Fuego

## D-001 | Hero Seed Selection | Design
- **Choice:** `S-AmbientAtmosphere` as primary hero anchor with an editorial typography mutation layer.
- **Why:** Preserves cinematic depth and avoids the already-overused split-agency hero.
- **Risk:** Can degrade into an atmospheric blur wall if the visual layers stay too close in luminance.

## D-002 | Navigation Direction | Design
- **Choice:** `N-Contextual` to preserve theme shifts across dark/light/accent sections.
- **Why:** It supports a stronger brand-driven motion system than a static pill.

## D-003 | Retry Policy | Quality
- **Choice:** Auto-retry the hero until depth and craft both land at MEDIUM+.
- **Why:** The current hero is close enough to threshold that human intervention would be premature.
documents.reviewMarkdown:
# Review Summary — Atelier Fuego

## Current State

- Direction is on brand and clearly non-generic.
- Hero is one retry away from approval.
- Navigation direction is approved as a seed family.

## Quality Snapshot

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Observer final | 7.8 |
| Critic final | 7.4 |
| Combined score | 7.7 |
| Visual debt open | 7 |

## Next Action

Rebuild the hero mid-plane depth for tablet and rerun observer plus critic.

directions.label: Editorial Tension
directions.rationale: Ambient Atmosphere preserves cinematic depth while Contextual keeps the navigation authored into the page. Shared tags editorial keep hero and nav moving in the same visual language. Both stay viable on the dark scheme.

directions.label: Cinematic Frame
directions.rationale: Type Monument keeps the homepage type-led while Fullscreen Overlay turns navigation into a ceremony. Shared tags editorial, luxury keep hero and nav moving in the same visual language. Both stay viable on the dark scheme.

directions.label: Ambient Signal
directions.rationale: Cinematic Curtain adds launch drama while Glass Blur keeps the header premium and atmospheric. Shared tags luxury, ambient keep hero and nav moving in the same visual language. Both stay viable on the dark scheme.

selection.rationale: Ambient Atmosphere preserves cinematic depth while Contextual keeps the navigation authored into the page. Shared tags editorial keep hero and nav moving in the same visual language. Both stay viable on the dark scheme.

rules.text: No aprobar heroes con visual estructural invisible sobre fondo oscuro
Toda seccion debe exponer una decision compositiva visible en screenshot
Priorizar seeds con identidad tipografica fuerte antes que layouts neutros
No aprobar si contrast = FAIL aunque el score creativo sea alto

visualDebt.issue: Primary glow and particle field are still too close in luminance on tablet.
Section label feels slightly detached from CTA rhythm at 768px.

notes: The hero already feels branded and non-generic, but the ambient depth still reads softer than the desired award-level tension.
Typography is doing the heavy lifting successfully; the next iteration should make the mid-plane visual more legible at tablet width.
The current CTA cluster is credible, but the section still needs one sharper detail to feel fully resolved.

issues.message: The visual hierarchy is clear, but the atmosphere still absorbs too much contrast on 768px.
The hero needs one more high-frequency detail so the lower third does not flatten after the first read.

retryInstructions: Increase hero mid-plane separation on tablet by widening the luminance gap between particle field and glow cluster.
Add one sharper craft signal to the lower third without turning the CTA area into a SaaS control bar.

items.message: Tablet depth still lacks enough separation between atmosphere and mid-plane visual.
Section rhythm repeats the hero's density pattern too closely.
Section label cadence and CTA hover timing are slightly detached.
